field.label: Admin User
field.help: Admin username for the AdGuard Home web interface. Managed by the plugin (AdGuard Home has no UI to change it).

field.label: Admin Password
field.help: Admin password for the AdGuard Home web interface. Enter a value to set or change it; leave blank to keep the current password. Stored in config.xml and applied (bcrypt-hashed) to AdGuardHome.yaml on Save. If left blank on first setup, the web UI has no login until you set one.

field.label: Web Interface Port
field.help: TCP port for the AdGuard Home admin web interface. Set here (AdGuard Home has no UI to change its own admin port); also used for the "Open AdGuard Home" link below.

field.label: DNS Port
field.help: UDP/TCP port AdGuard Home listens on for DNS. Keep this a high port (e.g. 5353) so AdGuard Home runs unprivileged and does not clash with Unbound on 53 — point clients or Unbound at this port. (You can also change it in AdGuard Home's own UI, but the plugin re-asserts this value on Save.)

field.label: Run As User
field.help: System user used to run AdGuard Home. Defaults to the dedicated, unprivileged "adguardhome" account (least privilege). Because the plugin seeds a config before first start, AdGuard Home never needs root — leave this as "adguardhome" unless you have a specific reason to change it.

field.label: Configuration File
field.help: Path to AdGuardHome.yaml. AdGuard Home owns and rewrites this file; the plugin seeds it and rewrites only the managed keys (admin user/password, web port, DNS port). Change only if you know what you are doing.

field.label: Working Directory
field.help: Directory for AdGuard Home runtime data (filters, query log, statistics, sessions database).

field.label: Extra Options
field.help: Additional command-line arguments passed to the AdGuard Home binary.
